name: Pan-Roasted Veal Chops with Sage and Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper -- to taste
1 cup parboiled pearl onions, (frozen are fine)
12 ounces fresh, cleaned, sliced mushrooms such as Cremini, Portobello, Shiitake, Oyster, or any combination
2 ounces Prosciutto ham, thinly sliced, julienned
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal chops with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a large, deep skillet over medium heat.
3. Add the veal chops and brown 3 to 4 minutes on each side until nicely browned. Remove from pan.
4. In the same pan, sauté onions for 3 to 4 minutes or until mushrooms begin to wilt.
5. Add white wine and reduce until it evaporates.
6. Add veal stock, sage, and Prosciutto; bring to a boil.
7. Return veal chops to pan and cover, leaving lid slightly ajar. Reduce heat to low and cook additional 10 minutes.
8. Serve chops on individual plates with sauce spooned over each. Accompany with Roasted Garlic and Roasted Tomato and Goat Cheese Salad.
Yield: 4 servings
